mission.title: coredns: plugin/azure: add support for private zones
mission.description: plugin/azure: add support for private zones. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current coredns deployment
steps.description:
Verify your coredns version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
helm list -n coredns 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working coredns installation.

steps.title: Review coredns configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant coredns configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl get configmap -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=coredns
```
Azure supports hosting private dns zones, `plugin/azure` should support it. 
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/dns/private-dns-overview

steps.title: Apply the fix for plugin/azure: add support for private zones
steps.description:
Fixes a bug in `azure` plugin introduced during refactor

Yes, changes the syntax for `azure` plugin block

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n coredns -l app.kubernetes.io/name=coredns
kubectl get events -n coredns --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "plugin/azure: add support for private zones" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Fixes a bug in `azure` plugin introduced during refactor

Yes, changes the syntax for `azure` plugin block

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with coredns installed or the issue environment reproducible.
